title: Artists and AI Art Generation
passage:
Rutkowski is a Polish digital artist who uses classical painting styles to create dreamy fantasy landscapes. He has made illustrations for games such as Sony's Horizon Forbidden West, Ubisoft's Anno, Dungeons & Dragons, and Magic: The Gathering. And he has become a sudden hit in the new world of text-to-image AI generation.

His distinctive style is now one of the most commonly used prompts in the new open-source AI art generator Stable Diffusion, which was launched late last month. The tool, along with other popular image-generation AI models, allows anyone to create impressive images based on text prompts. For example, type in "Wizard with sword and a glowing orb of magic fire fights a fierce dragon Greg Rutkowski," and the system will produce something that looks not a million miles away from works in Rutkowski's style.

But these open-source programs are built by scraping images from the Internet, often without permission and proper attribution to artists. As a result, they are raising tricky questions about ethics and copyright. And artists like Rutkowski have had enough.

According to the website Lexica, which tracks over 10 million images and prompts generated by Stable Diffusion, Rutkowski's name has been used as a prompt around 93,000 times. Some of the world's most famous artists, such as Michelangelo, Pablo Picasso, and Leonardo da Vinci, brought up around 2,000 prompts each or less. Rutkowski's name also features as a prompt thousands of times in the Discord of another text-to-image generator, Midjourney. Rutkowski was initially surprised but thought it might be a good way to reach new audiences. Then he tried searching for his name to see if a piece he had worked on had been published. The online search brought back work that had his name attached to it but was not his.

"It has been just a month. What about in a year? I probably will not be able to find my work out there because the internet will be flooded with AI art," Rutkowski says. "That is concerning."

"There is a coalition growing within artist industries to figure out how to tackle or mitigate this," says Ortiz. The group is in its early days of mobilization, which could involve pushing for new policies or regulation. One suggestion is that AI models could be trained on images in the public domain, and AI companies could forge partnerships with museums and artists, Ortiz says.

questions.questionText: What can be learned about Rutkowski from the first two paragraphs?
questions.question: What can be learned about Rutkowski from the first two paragraphs?

options.A: He is enthusiastic about AI generation painting.
options.B: He is popular with the users of an AI art generator.
options.C: He attracts admiration from other illustrators.
options.D: He specializes in classical painting digitalization.

questions.location: His distinctive style is now one of the most commonly used prompts in the new open-source AI art generator Stable Diffusion.
questions.explanation: 前两段说明 Rutkowski 的风格在 AI 作图工具中被频繁用作提示词，因此他受到 AI 作图用户欢迎。
questions.thinkingMethod: popular with users 对应 commonly used prompts。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 是后文态度误读；C 原文未说其他插画师崇拜；D 把 uses classical painting styles 误读成专门做古典画数字化。
questions.commonMistake: 偷换概念：把风格被 AI 用户大量使用误解成作者本人支持 AI 绘画。

questions.questionText: The problem with open-source AI art generators is that they ________.
questions.question: The problem with open-source AI art generators is that they ________.

options.A: lack flexibility in responding to prompts
options.C: make unauthorized use of online images

questions.location: These open-source programs are built by scraping images from the Internet, often without permission and proper attribution to artists.
questions.explanation: 问题在于这些程序抓取网络图片时常常没有获得许可，也没有给艺术家恰当署名。
questions.thinkingMethod: without permission 对应 unauthorized use。
questions.trapAnalysis: A、B 原文无依据；D 把 image scraping 误读为收集用户信息。
questions.commonMistake: 对象错位：把未经许可使用图片看成未经许可收集用户信息。

questions.question: After searching online, Rutkowski found ________.

options.A: a unique way to reach audiences
options.B: a new method to identify AI images

questions.location: The online search brought back work that had his name attached to it but was not his.
questions.explanation: 他搜索自己的名字后发现一些作品带着他的名字，但并不是他的作品。
questions.thinkingMethod: had his name attached to it but was not his 对应 AI-generated work bearing his name。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 是他最初的想法；B、D 原文没有说搜索发现了这些。
questions.commonMistake: 时间线混乱：把 initially surprised but thought it might be a good way 当成搜索后的发现。

questions.questionText: According to Ortiz, AI companies are advised to ________.
questions.question: According to Ortiz, AI companies are advised to ________.

options.A: campaign for new policies or regulations
options.B: offer their services to public institutions
options.C: strengthen their relationships with AI users
options.D: adopt a different strategy for AI model training

questions.location: One suggestion is that AI models could be trained on images in the public domain, and AI companies could forge partnerships with museums and artists.
questions.explanation: Ortiz 提到 AI 模型可用公有领域图片训练，并与博物馆、艺术家合作，本质是改变训练数据和训练策略。
questions.thinkingMethod: trained on images in the public domain 对应 different strategy for model training。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 是艺术行业联盟可能推动的行动，不是 AI 公司建议；B、C 表述偏离原文对象。
questions.commonMistake: 主语错位：把 coalition 的行动误当成 AI companies 的建议。

questions.questionText: What is the text mainly about?
questions.question: What is the text mainly about?

options.A: Artists' responses to AI art generation.
options.B: AI's expanded role in artistic creation.
options.C: Privacy issues in the application of AI.

questions.location: They are raising tricky questions about ethics and copyright. And artists like Rutkowski have had enough.
questions.explanation: 文章以 Rutkowski 为例，讨论艺术家对 AI 作图中未经许可使用风格和图片的担忧及应对。
questions.thinkingMethod: 主旨题要综合人物案例、问题和最后的行业行动。
questions.trapAnalysis: B 过宽且偏正面；C 不是隐私问题；D 没有呈现双方对立观点。
questions.commonMistake: 范围扩大：把艺术家维权和反应扩展成 AI 发展的一般争论。

vocabulary.sentence: His distinctive style is now one of the most commonly used prompts.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 他独特的风格如今是最常用的提示词之一。

vocabulary.sentence: His style is one of the most commonly used prompts.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 他的风格是最常用的提示词之一。
vocabulary.explanation: AI 语境中 prompt 指输入给模型的提示。

vocabulary.sentence: These programs are built by scraping images from the Internet.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 这些程序通过从互联网抓取图片构建。
vocabulary.explanation: 互联网语境中 scrape 指自动抓取数据。

vocabulary.sentence: Often without permission and proper attribution to artists.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 常常没有获得许可，也没有给艺术家恰当署名。
vocabulary.explanation: attribution 指标明来源或归属。

vocabulary.sentence: They are raising tricky questions about ethics and copyright.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 它们引发了有关伦理和版权的棘手问题。
vocabulary.explanation: ethics 指道德原则和伦理问题。

vocabulary.sentence: They are raising tricky questions about ethics and copyright.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 它们引发了有关伦理和版权的棘手问题。
vocabulary.explanation: copyright 指作品的法律权利。

vocabulary.sentence: The internet will be flooded with AI art.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 互联网将充斥着 AI 艺术作品。
vocabulary.explanation: be flooded with 表示大量充满。
vocabulary.note: 熟词僻义，不是实际洪水。

vocabulary.sentence: There is a coalition growing within artist industries.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 艺术行业内部正在形成一个联盟。
vocabulary.explanation: coalition 指为共同目标形成的联盟。

vocabulary.sentence: The coalition aims to figure out how to tackle or mitigate this.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 该联盟旨在弄清如何处理或缓解这一问题。
vocabulary.explanation: mitigate 常用于减少负面影响。

familiarWordsWithNewMeanings.sentence: His distinctive style is now one of the most commonly used prompts.
familiarWordsWithNewMeanings.explanation: prompt 在 AI 语境中不是“迅速的”，而是输入给模型的文字提示。

articleStructure: 介绍 Rutkowski 及其风格流行；说明 AI 工具如何使用提示词生成类似风格；指出图片抓取的伦理和版权问题；展示 Rutkowski 的担忧；提出行业联盟和训练策略建议。
